summary.intent: Add a user_message broadcast event to the chat system across backend (Rust), frontend (JS), and iOS client, then enable the previously-skipped e2e tests for this feature.
summary.outcome: Successfully implemented the broadcast in send.rs, added frontend and iOS handlers, updated e2e tests to remove fixme markers, passed all 96 unit tests, and pushed the commit.

repo: The chat broadcast pipeline spans: crates/chat/src/service/chat_impl/send.rs (Rust backend), crates/web/src/assets/js/websocket.js (web frontend), apps/ios/Sources/Networking/ChatEventTypes.swift (iOS), and crates/web/ui/e2e/specs/chat-api-message.spec.js (e2e tests).
Variables text, session_key, and client_seq are all in scope at the broadcast site in send.rs.
The actual broadcast does not include a messageIndex field — e2e test payloads must omit it.
Pre-existing Rust format issues exist in the repo unrelated to this change; clippy passes clean.
AnyCodable unresolved diagnostic in Swift is a pre-existing dependency issue, not introduced by this change.

code.finding: text is a String containing the user's text content; session_key and client_seq are also in scope at the broadcast call site.
Tests were previously gated with test.fixme() pending this broadcast implementation; messageIndex must not be included in expected payloads.
channel_user broadcast pattern lives here and served as the reference for implementing the new user_message broadcast.

workflow: Greptile review threads must be explicitly resolved via API after pushing fixes; the check re-runs automatically on the new commit but may need manual triggering.
Greploop tooling reports iteration count, confidence score, and resolved/remaining comment counts as a structured summary.
Running cargo test for just the affected crate (chat) is sufficient when changes are scoped to that crate's broadcast logic.

friction: Greptile check did not appear automatically on the new commit and required manual triggering.
Pre-existing Rust formatting issues (unrelated to the change) created noise when running format checks.
Multiple reads of websocket.js were needed before editing, suggesting uncertainty about the correct insertion point.
open_items: Pre-existing Rust formatting issues across the repo remain unaddressed.
Unresolved AnyCodable Swift dependency diagnostic is a known pre-existing issue with no fix applied.
No dedicated unit tests for the new broadcast call in send.rs — coverage relies entirely on e2e tests.
